name: TPC
description:
TPC (Texture) is the main texture format for KotOR I & II. It stores 2D textures with optional mipmaps, optional S3TC (DXT) compression, and an optional trailing TXI block for rendering hints.

Layout: a fixed 128-byte header, then raw pixel data for all faces and mip levels. The pixel data has no stored length—you must compute its size from the header (encoding type, mip count, dimensions, and face count) using the steps in 'How to compute image data size' below. Immediately after the last byte of pixel data, the file may contain an optional TXI block: a single null-terminated text string whose syntax is defined in the TXI format spec. If there are no bytes left after the pixel data, there is no TXI.

TPCHeader.description: Fixed 128-byte header. First six fields define dimensions, encoding, and mip count; the rest is reserved. dataSize: when 0, texture is uncompressed and data size is derived from width×height×bytesPerPixel; when non-zero, texture is S3TC-compressed and dataSize is the byte size of the top mip level for that encoding. If height/width == 6 the texture is treated as a cubemap (6 faces).

fields.name: dataSize
fields.description: Compressed top mip size in bytes, or 0 for uncompressed. When 0, pixel data size is width×height×bytesPerPixel per mip (bytesPerPixel from Encoding).

fields.name: alphaTest
fields.description: Alpha test/reference value used by the engine when sampling the texture.

fields.name: height
fields.description: Texture height in pixels. If height/width == 6, file is a cubemap (6 faces).

fields.name: encoding
fields.description: Pixel encoding: 1=GRAY, 2=RGB, 4=RGBA, 12=BGRA; when dataSize≠0 only 2 (DXT1) and 4 (DXT5) are used.

fields.name: mipMapCount
fields.description: Number of mip levels; effective value is max(1, this).

fields.name: reserved
fields.description: Reserved; rest of header to 128 bytes.

Encoding.description: Pixel encoding and compression. GRAY/RGB/RGBA/BGRA are uncompressed when dataSize is 0; RGB and RGBA with dataSize≠0 mean S3TC DXT1 and DXT5 respectively.

sections.name: header
sections.description: Fixed 128-byte header. Read dataSize, alphaTest, width, height, encoding, mipMapCount; use encoding and dataSize to determine compressed vs uncompressed and bytes per pixel. Cubemap when height/width == 6.

sections.name: imageData
sections.description: Raw pixel data for all faces and mip levels. Size is not stored in the file—you must compute it from the header using the steps in 'How to compute image data size' below. Starts at file offset 128. Once you have the computed imageDataLength (in bytes), image data occupies the range [128, 128 + imageDataLength). The next section (TXI) begins at 128 + imageDataLength.

sections.name: txi
sections.description: Optional TXI block: rendering hints (blend mode, filtering, procedure type, etc.) stored as a single null-terminated text string. Present only when there is at least one byte after the image data. Start: immediately after the last byte of image data (file offset 128 + imageDataLength). End: last byte of the file. Content: the bytes in this range form a C-style string—characters from the start up to (but not including) the first 0x00 byte; the encoding is typically ASCII or Windows-1252. The syntax of the text (keywords, line format, and meaning of each directive) is defined in a separate TXI format spec (e.g. txi-format); that document is the authoritative source for the full TXI grammar. If imageDataLength equals (file size - 128), this block has zero length and there is no TXI.

layoutSteps.title: How to compute image data size
steps: Effective mip count: M = max(1, header.mipMapCount).
Face count: if header.height / header.width == 6 then F = 6 (cubemap), else F = 1.

Bytes per pixel or block size from encoding: If header.dataSize == 0 (uncompressed), use bytesPerPixel = 1 for GRAY, 3 for RGB, 4 for RGBA or BGRA. If header.dataSize != 0 (compressed), use minDataSize = 8 for RGB (DXT1) or 16 for RGBA (DXT5); block count per mip = ceil((W+3)/4) * ceil((H+3)/4).

For each face 0..F-1, for each mip level 0..M-1: start with W = header.width, H = header.height, and (for compressed) dataSize = header.dataSize. Uncompressed chunk size = W * H * bytesPerPixel. Compressed chunk size = max(minDataSize, block_count * minDataSize) with block_count = ceil((W+3)/4) * ceil((H+3)/4). Add that chunk size to the running total. Then set W = max(W/2, 1), H = max(H/2, 1), and for compressed set dataSize = max(dataSize/4, minDataSize); repeat for the next mip.
The total of all chunk sizes is imageDataLength. Image data occupies file bytes 128 to 128 + imageDataLength - 1 inclusive.

Optional TXI: If file size > 128 + imageDataLength, then bytes from 128 + imageDataLength to end of file are the TXI block. Read as a null-terminated string (content ends at the first 0x00). The full TXI format (keywords and syntax) is defined in the separate TXI format spec.
